title: Environmental Engineer
description: Environmental Engineers in India work on designing and implementing solutions to reduce pollution and waste. They conduct site assessments, develop environmental impact statements, and oversee the implementation of environmental regulations. They often work with government agencies, private companies, and non-profit organizations to develop and enforce environmental policies and regulations.
degree_name: Bachelor of Technology in Environmental Engineering
top_private_colleges: Birla Institute of Technology and Science (BITS) Pilani
Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology

career_progression.year_0_2: Assist in conducting site assessments and developing environmental impact statements. Work under the supervision of senior engineers.
career_progression.year_3_5: Lead site assessments and develop environmental impact statements. Oversee the implementation of environmental regulations.
career_progression.year_6_10: Develop and implement environmental policies and regulations. Lead teams of engineers and technicians.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start a business providing environmental consulting services or developing sustainable products.
career_progression.global_pathway: Work with international organizations or companies in countries with strong environmental regulations, such as the US or EU.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is around ₹4-5 lakhs per annum. Working hours are long, and the job can be stressful.
common_misconceptions: Environmental Engineers only work for government agencies.
